name: Agno
description: Agno (formerly Phidata) is a lightweight, blazing-fast Python framework for multimodal AI agents. Claims ~10,000x faster instantiation than LangGraph with ~3.75 KiB per agent. Supports 23+ model providers, multi-agent teams, built-in Agentic RAG, and 40+ tools. 20K+ GitHub stars.
oneLiner: Lightweight, blazing-fast Python framework for multimodal AI agents with model-agnostic design and multi-agent teams.
pricing: Open-source (MPL-2.0, free); Agno Cloud: Free (5 agents), Pro ($29/mo), Enterprise (custom)
capabilities: 23+ model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Bedrock, Groq, Ollama)
Agentic RAG with multiple vector stores
Knowledge bases (PDF, URL, text, JSON, CSV)
AgentOS — stateless FastAPI runtime for production deployment
Control Plane UI for monitoring and management
100+ integrations with memory, knowledge, and guardrails

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Code-first Python: pip install, import Agent, configure model + tools + knowledge + memory, run. 3-5 lines for a basic agent. Teams compose agents with a leader. Agno Cloud adds web playground for testing and monitoring. Optimized for Python developers wanting max control with minimal boilerplate.
strengths: 100+ cookbook examples for diverse use cases
weaknesses: No kill switches, guardrails, or approval gates built in
Cloud is monitoring-only, not managed execution
No cost tracking or token metering
notableFeatures: Agno Cloud playground with session replay

recentDevelopments: 2026-03: v2.5.13 — ReliabilityEval for agent evaluation, enhanced AgentOS APIs, Slack interface improvements
2026-04: v2.5.14 — latest release with continued rapid development (commits within 24 hours)
2026: 39,100+ GitHub stars — one of the fastest-growing agent frameworks
2026: Three integrated layers — Python SDK, AgentOS (FastAPI runtime), Control Plane UI
Jan 2025: Rebranded from Phidata to Agno
2025 Q1: Published ~10,000x speed benchmarks vs LangGraph
2025: Rapid releases (multiple/week), new model providers and tools

deploymentOptions.notes: Open-source Python framework. Runs wherever Python runs. No managed cloud.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: 3-5 lines of Python for a basic agent. Excellent templates. No visual builder. Under 10 min for Python devs.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud playground with session replay. show_tool_calls for reasoning. Structured logging.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Excellent docs, 100+ cookbook examples, YouTube, blog. Clear migration from Phidata.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Instant: change code, re-run. No build step. Cloud playground for rapid prototyping.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: First-class Agent Teams: coordinate (parallel), route (delegate), collaborate (sequential). Dynamic selection.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Agents can consult humans via custom tools. No built-in handoff UX or evidence packets.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud supports team workspaces. Code integrates with Git. No CI/CD pipelines.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: 100+ cookbook examples covering RAG, multi-agent, search, data analysis, coding, finance.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: 40+ tools: search, SQL, S3, email, Slack, GitHub. Knowledge base connectors. No enterprise ERP/CRM.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: OpenAI/Anthropic tool-use. Early MCP support. No GraphQL/gRPC.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Basic API key. OAuth via tools. SSO on Enterprise only.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Strong Agentic RAG. Multiple vector stores. Document readers. Configurable chunking.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: No built-in kill switch or behavioral guardrails system.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: No approval gate system. Manual HITL implementation required.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud logs sessions. No tamper-proof logging or compliance certs.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Basic team/user management on paid tiers. No granular RBAC.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Deploy anywhere Python runs. Cloud is monitoring-only. Self-hosted primary. No managed execution.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Exceptional per-agent performance. Async. Horizontal scaling is developer's responsibility.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud provides session monitoring and history. No OpenTelemetry or enterprise observability.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: ~20K GitHub stars, growing fast post-rebrand. Active Discord. Developer mindshare increasing.

scoringRationale: 70/100 overall. Strongest: Creation (18/20). Weakest: Governance (9/20). Breakdown: Creation (18/20), Orchestration (15/20), Integration (14/20), Governance (9/20), Operations (14/20).
targetUser: Python developers wanting fast, minimal agent creation

communityBuzz: Rebrand from Phidata to Agno generating fresh interest — 20K+ GitHub stars
10,000x faster instantiation benchmark gets attention but questioned methodology
Praised as cleanest Python API in the agent framework space
Seed-stage funding concerns — can they compete long-term with well-funded competitors?

walkthrough.title: Multi-Agent Research Team with Agno

steps.title: Define a Research Agent with Tools and Knowledge
steps.description: Create an agent in Python with Agno's Agent class — assign it a role (e.g., market researcher), attach web search and database tools, and connect a knowledge base of industry reports. Agno's session-based memory persists context across interactions.

works: Minimal API surface means less to learn — philosophy of simplicity
Clean Python code that reads like pseudocode
Multi-modal support (text, image, audio) without framework bloat
doesntWork: Minimal framework means building everything yourself — no batteries included
Documentation is thin compared to LangChain — fewer examples and tutorials
Community is small — fewer Stack Overflow answers and community solutions
pmCritique.userPsychology: Developers who have been burned by LangChain complexity feel relief when they see Agno — it is the framework for people who do not want a framework. But that minimalism means more DIY work.

steps.title: Compose a Multi-Agent Team
steps.description: Build a team of specialized agents: a researcher gathers data, an analyst evaluates findings, and a writer drafts the report. Agno's Team class handles coordination — agents share context and delegate sub-tasks automatically.

works: Agent team composition is explicit and readable
Tool integration is straightforward — standard Python functions
Memory implementation is simple and transparent
doesntWork: No visual debugger or execution trace UI
Team coordination patterns are limited compared to CrewAI or LangGraph
No built-in persistence — must implement yourself
pmCritique.userPsychology: Control-oriented developers prefer Agno because they can read every line of what happens. But the absence of visual debugging tools means complex multi-agent flows require printf debugging.

steps.title: Execute with Real-Time Reasoning
steps.description: Run the team on a research query. Watch agents collaborate with concurrent tool calls — Agno's async-first architecture enables parallel execution. The researcher and analyst work simultaneously, merging their findings.

works: Playground web UI provides quick experimentation environment
Structured output with Pydantic validation ensures type safety
Async execution support for production performance
doesntWork: Playground is basic — no conversation history or session management
No deployment tooling — must containerize and deploy yourself
No managed hosting option — fully self-serve
pmCritique.userPsychology: The playground bridges the gap between reading docs and building something real — it is the try-before-you-commit experience. But once you commit, you are on your own for deployment.

steps.title: Monitor on Agno Cloud
steps.description: Track the team's performance on Agno Cloud's dashboard — agent-level metrics, tool call latency, token usage, and session replays. Identify bottlenecks and optimize agent instructions based on real execution data.

pmVerdict.summary: Agno is the speed demon of the agent framework space — built for developers who need sub-second agent initialization and multimodal support. The lightweight API and model-agnostic design make prototyping fast. But it's early-stage (small community, limited docs) and competes against LangGraph and CrewAI with far less ecosystem maturity. Best for: developers who know exactly what they want and need raw speed.
strengths: Fastest agent initialization in the market — sub-second cold starts
Multimodal support (text, image, audio, video) from day one
Lightweight — no heavy dependencies, minimal overhead
risks: Small community (<5K stars) — limited support and examples
No visual builder or no-code path — developers only
Competing against well-funded LangGraph and CrewAI with fraction of ecosystem
No enterprise features — no RBAC, no audit, no compliance

ctoVerdict.summary: Agno is a technical bet on speed over ecosystem. The lightweight architecture is appealing for latency-sensitive applications, but the small team and community create bus-factor risk. No enterprise features, no compliance certifications, no support SLA. Use for internal tooling or experimental projects — not for customer-facing production agents.
strengths: Minimal dependency footprint reduces supply chain risk
Open-source MIT license — full control, no vendor lock-in
Clean API design — easy to evaluate and understand quickly
Multimodal support positions well for future agent capabilities
dealBreakers: Small team — bus factor risk for production dependency
No governance, compliance, or audit capabilities
Limited integration ecosystem — must build connectors yourself

coverageGaps: Live product demo (no comprehensive YouTube tutorials exist)

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: No video demo — score based on documentation review and code examples
scoringAlignment.gap: Need live walkthrough to verify claimed speed and developer experience

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: Multi-agent patterns documented but not demonstrated visually
Model provider support documented, tool creation API reviewed
scoringAlignment.gap: Integration breadth claims unverified via video

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: No governance features visible in any available content
scoringAlignment.gap: Score relies entirely on documentation — no visual evidence
High operations score needs benchmarking evidence

youtubeReviews.title: Agno Tutorial | Anyone can now build AI Agents with Python!
Agno Framework Tutorial: Build Faster AI Agents with Concurrent Tool Calls

coreCompetence.bestAt: The lightest-weight Python agent framework — minimal abstraction layer that stays close to the metal while providing just enough structure for multi-agent workflows.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Python developers who want agent capabilities without heavy framework overhead. Teams that find LangChain too opinionated and want more control.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins against LangChain/LangGraph on simplicity and lightweight footprint. Wins on developer control — less magic, more transparency. Loses on ecosystem breadth and enterprise tooling. No visual UI, no managed platform.
